novel_features.name: Pacing observatory
novel_features.description: See which campaigns are under- or over-pacing against their budget, not just current spend.
novel_features.rationale: Joins each campaign's budget with its delivery spend over time to compute pace, which the dashboard and warehouse connectors do not do in one call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to catch campaigns that will overspend or underdeliver before the budget cycle ends.

novel_features.name: Delivery drift
novel_features.description: Track CTR, CPM, and spend drift week-over-week for a campaign and flag when performance degrades.
novel_features.rationale: Requires persisted per-window delivery history that no single GraphQL call returns at once.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to catch a slowly degrading campaign before it wastes budget.

novel_features.name: Bottleneck
novel_features.description: Rank the highest-spend campaigns by worst ROAS or CPA, with a reason column.
novel_features.rationale: Local join across campaigns and delivery to surface where budget is wasted.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to find where ad budget is being wasted.

novel_features.name: Stale campaigns
novel_features.description: Find active campaigns with zero delivery in the last N days.
novel_features.rationale: Knowing a campaign delivered nothing requires persisted delivery history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to find live-but-not-delivering campaigns.

narrative.headline: The first agent-native StackAdapt CLI: pacing, delivery-drift, and budget-waste analysis the dashboard and warehouse connectors can't answer in one call.
narrative.value_prop: StackAdapt is a programmatic advertising platform. This read-only CLI queries your advertisers, campaigns, ads, audiences, and delivery reporting straight from the StackAdapt GraphQL API and answers questions the dashboard shows one screen at a time and the warehouse connectors only dump raw rows for: which campaigns are under-pacing, whose CTR has drifted, where budget is being wasted. It does not change anything in StackAdapt (read-only); it tells you what your campaigns are doing.
narrative.auth_narrative:
This CLI uses the StackAdapt GraphQL API, which needs a GraphQL API token (the legacy REST key will not work). To get set up:

1. Ask your StackAdapt account manager for a GraphQL API token if you do not have one.
2. Export it so the CLI can read it:
   `export STACKADAPT_API_TOKEN=your-token`
3. Run `stackadapt-pp-cli doctor` to confirm it connects.

The token is sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. The CLI is read-only and never modifies your campaigns.

quickstart.comment: Confirms the CLI is installed and shows what a health check verifies (no token needed).
Lists your advertisers — the top of the hierarchy and your starting point.
List campaigns with status and budget metadata.
The headline answer: which campaigns are under- or over-pacing against budget.
Where your ad budget is being wasted — highest spend, worst performance.

troubleshoots.fix: Make sure STACKADAPT_API_TOKEN is a GraphQL API token (not a legacy REST key); request one from your StackAdapt account manager.
Your token may be scoped to a different account; run `stackadapt-pp-cli account` to see what it can access.
StackAdapt rate-limits the API; the CLI backs off and retries — re-run after a moment or narrow the date window.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI to analyze StackAdapt campaigns you already run: check pacing against budget, find under-performing or non-delivering campaigns, spot CTR/spend drift, and pull delivery reports. It is the right tool for read-only reporting and analysis, especially for agents and scripts that want JSON output and a local cache.
anti_triggers: Creating, editing, pausing, or deleting campaigns, ads, or segments — this CLI is read-only; use the StackAdapt UI or the official SDK.
Uploading audiences or creatives — out of scope.
Real-time bidding or pixel firing — not what this CLI does.

recipes.title: Find under-pacing campaigns
recipes.explanation: Returns each campaign's pace (actual vs expected spend) so you can fix budget delivery before the cycle ends.

recipes.title: Catch a degrading campaign
recipes.explanation: Shows CTR/CPM/spend drift week-over-week and flags when performance slips.

recipes.title: Trim a verbose report for an agent
recipes.explanation: Delivery reports are large; --select with dotted paths returns only the fields an agent needs, saving context.

recipes.title: Find where budget is wasted
recipes.explanation: Ranks high-spend campaigns by worst ROAS/CPA with a reason column.

trigger_phrases: are my stackadapt campaigns on budget
where is my ad budget being wasted
